_BMW CARS SALES REPORT_
By: Reyad Aly

1. Objectives ¶

  1. Number of sales per year
  2. Total revenue per year
  3. Number of sales per region
  4. Total revenue per region
  5. Number of sales per model
  6. Avg Price per model across the regions
  7. Total revenue by Top 5 Models and Colors
  8. Total revenue by Fuel Type (%)
  9. Total Revenue Distribution by Mileage_KM (Grouped) and Transmission
  10. Pairplot of Variables
  11. Top 5 Engine Sizes by their Sales Volume Distribution
  12. Price Distribution by Sales Classification

2. Data Loading & Cleaning

In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

3. Data Calculations ¶

data["Total"]=data["Price_USD"]*data["Sales_Volume"]
data

In [20]:
ranges = [0, 50000, 100000, 150000, 500000]
mileage_labels = ["0-50k", "50-100k", "100-150k", "+150k"]
data["Mileage_Category"] = pd.cut(data["Mileage_KM"], bins=ranges, labels=mileage_labels, right=False)
data["Mileage_Category"].value_counts()

4. Data Visualization ¶

In [21]:
yearly_revenue = data.groupby("Year")["Total"].sum()
yearly_sales= data.groupby("Year")["Sales_Volume"].sum()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 10), facecolor="#1A1A1A")
chart1 = plt.subplot(2, 1, 1)
chart1.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
x1 = yearly_revenue.index
y1 = yearly_revenue.values
chart1.plot(x1, y1 / 1e12, marker="o", color="#3399CC", linewidth = 3, markersize = 8)
chart1.tick_params(axis="both", colors="white")
chart1.grid(False)
chart1.set_xlabel("Year", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart1.set_ylabel("Total Revenue in Trillions (USD)", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart1.set_title("Total Revenue by Year",weight="bold", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=10)

chart2 = plt.subplot(2, 1, 2)
chart2.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
x2 = yearly_sales.index
y2 = yearly_sales.values
chart2.fill_between(x2, y2 / 1e6, color="#3399CC", alpha=0.5)  
chart2.plot(x2, y2 / 1e6, color="#3399CC", linewidth=2)
chart2.set_ylim((yearly_sales.min()*0.99)/ 1e6, (yearly_sales.max()*1.01)/ 1e6)
chart2.tick_params(axis="both", colors="white")
chart2.grid(False)
chart2.set_xlabel("Year", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart2.set_ylabel("Total Sales Volume in Millions", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart2.set_title("Total Sales Volume by Year", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=10, weight="bold")

plt.subplots_adjust(hspace=0.4)
In [22]:
regions_sale = data.groupby("Region")["Sales_Volume"].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).reset_index()
regions_sale["Sales_Volume2"] = regions_sale['Sales_Volume'] / 1e6
x_min_regionssales = regions_sale["Sales_Volume2"].min()
x_max_regionssales = regions_sale["Sales_Volume2"].max()

regions_revenue = data.groupby("Region")["Total"].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).reset_index()
regions_revenue["Revenue_Plot"] = regions_revenue["Total"] / 1e12
x_min_regionsrevenue = regions_revenue["Revenue_Plot"].min()
x_max_regionsrevenue = regions_revenue["Revenue_Plot"].max()


plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7), facecolor="#1A1A1A")
chart3 = plt.subplot(1,2,1)
chart3.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
sns.barplot(data=regions_sale, y="Region", x="Sales_Volume2", palette='Blues_d',estimator=np.sum)
chart3.tick_params(axis="both", colors="white")
chart3.set_xlim(x_min_regionssales*0.99, x_max_regionssales*1.01)
chart3.set_xlabel("Total Sales in Millions", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart3.set_ylabel("Region", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart3.set_title("Total Sales by Region",weight="bold", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=10)

chart4 = plt.subplot(1, 2, 2)
chart4.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
sns.barplot(data=regions_revenue, y="Region", x="Revenue_Plot", palette='Blues_d', ax=chart4)
chart4.tick_params(axis="both", colors="white")
chart4.set_xlim(x_min_regionsrevenue * 0.99, x_max_regionsrevenue * 1.01)
chart4.set_xlabel("Total Revenue in Trillions (USD)", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart4.set_ylabel("Region", fontsize=12, color="white") 
chart4.set_title("Total Revenue by Region", weight="bold", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=10)


plt.subplots_adjust(wspace=0.4)
In [23]:
model_sale = data.groupby("Model")["Sales_Volume"].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).reset_index()
model_sale["Sales_Volume3"] = model_sale['Sales_Volume'] / 1e6
x_min_modelsales = model_sale["Sales_Volume3"].min()
x_max_modelsales = model_sale["Sales_Volume3"].max()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7), facecolor="#1A1A1A")
chart5 = sns.barplot(data=model_sale, y="Model", x="Sales_Volume3", palette='Blues_d',estimator=np.sum)
chart5.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
chart5.tick_params(axis="both", colors="white")
chart5.set_xlim(x_min_modelsales*0.99, x_max_modelsales*1.01)
chart5.set_xlabel("Total Sales in Millions", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart5.set_ylabel("Model", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart5.set_title("Total Sales by Model",weight="bold", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=10)
plt.show()
In [24]:
model_avg_price = data.groupby("Model")["Price_USD"].mean().sort_values(ascending=False).reset_index()
x_min_modelprice = model_avg_price["Price_USD"].min()
x_max_modelprice = model_avg_price["Price_USD"].max()


plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7), facecolor="#1A1A1A")
chart6 = sns.barplot(data=model_avg_price, y="Model", x="Price_USD", palette='Blues_d')
chart6.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
chart6.set_xlim(x_min_modelprice*0.99, x_max_modelprice*1.01)
chart6.tick_params(axis="both", colors="white")
chart6.set_xlabel("Average Price (USD)", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart6.set_ylabel("Model", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart6.set_title("Average Price by Model Across the Regions", weight="bold", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=10)
plt.show()
In [25]:
top_models = data.groupby("Model")["Sales_Volume"].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).head(5).reset_index()
top_models["Sales_Volume4"] = top_models["Sales_Volume"] / 1e6
top_colors = data.groupby("Color")["Sales_Volume"].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).head(5).reset_index()
top_colors["Sales_Volume4"] = top_colors["Sales_Volume"] / 1e6
filtered_data = data[data["Model"].isin(top_models["Model"]) & data["Color"].isin(top_colors["Color"])]
filtered_data["Sales_Volume_Millions"] = filtered_data["Sales_Volume"] / 1e6

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7), facecolor="#1A1A1A")
chart7 = sns.histplot(data=filtered_data, x="Model", weights="Sales_Volume_Millions", hue="Color", 
                      multiple="stack", palette="Blues", linewidth=0.5, shrink=0.6)
chart7.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
chart7.tick_params(axis="both", colors="white")
chart7.set_xlabel("Model", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart7.set_ylabel("Total Sales Volume (Millions)", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart7.set_title("Total Sales by Top Models and Colors", weight="bold", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=10)
plt.show()

fuel_sales = data.groupby("Fuel_Type")["Sales_Volume"].sum()
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5), facecolor="#1A1A1A")
wedges, texts, autotexts = plt.pie(fuel_sales, labels=fuel_sales.index, autopct='%.1f%%', explode=[0.02,0.02,0.02,0.02],
                                   colors=['#F6FAFF', '#C7DAEE', '#2373B6', '#6DAED6'],textprops={'color': 'white'})

for pie_color in autotexts:
    pie_color.set_color('black')

plt.title("Total Sales by Fuel Type %", weight="bold", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=20)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

data["Total2"] = data["Total"] / 1e9

                        split=True, palette="Blues", estimator=np.sum)
chart8.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")

plt.xticks(color="white")
plt.yticks(color="white")
plt.show()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8), facecolor="#1A1A1A")
selected_columns = ["Sales_Volume", "Total", "Price_USD", "Engine_Size_L", "Mileage_KM"]
chart9 = sns.pairplot(data[selected_columns])
chart9.figure.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
for bg in chart9.axes.flatten():
    bg.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
    bg.tick_params(colors="white")
    bg.xaxis.label.set_color("white")
    bg.yaxis.label.set_color("white")

chart9.figure.suptitle("Pairplot of Key Variables", fontsize=14, color="white", weight="bold", y=1.02)
plt.show()

top_engines = data.groupby("Engine_Size_L")["Sales_Volume"].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).head(5).index
filtered_data2 = data[data["Engine_Size_L"].isin(top_engines)]

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7), facecolor="#1A1A1A")
chart10 = sns.boxplot(data=filtered_data2, x="Engine_Size_L", y="Sales_Volume", palette="Blues_d",
                      whiskerprops={'color': 'white'}, capprops={'color': 'white'})

chart10.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
chart10.tick_params(axis="both", colors="white")
chart10.set_xlabel("Engine Size (L)", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart10.set_ylabel("Sales Volume Distribution", fontsize=12, color="white")
chart10.set_title("Top 5 Engine Sizes - Sales Volume Distribution", weight="bold", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=10)

plt.show()

plt.figure(figsize=(12, 7), facecolor="#1A1A1A")
chart11 = sns.histplot(data=data, x="Price_USD", hue="Sales_Classification", 
                       multiple="dodge", bins=5, palette="Blues_d", shrink=0.8)

chart11.set_facecolor("#1A1A1A")
chart11.tick_params(axis="both", colors="white")
chart11.set_xlabel("Price (USD)", fontsize=12, color="white") 
chart11.set_ylabel("Frequency",fontsize=12, color="white")
chart11.set_title("Price Distribution by Sales Classification", 
                  weight="bold", fontsize=14, color="white", pad=10)

plt.show()


_RECOMMENDATION LETTER_


INTRODUCTION

This report analyzes BMW sales data from 2010 to 2024, covering revenue, sales volume, regional performance, model pricing, fuel type preferences, mileage categories, and engine size contribution to total revenue.


INSIGHTS

• Revenue peaked at 1.34 trillion in 2022 (from 1.26 trillion in 2010), a 6.5% increase over 14 years. Sales volume peaked at 17.9 million units in 2022. Both metrics show moderate growth with annual fluctuations.

• Regional distribution is nearly equal across six regions (Asia, Europe, North America, Middle East, Africa, South America), each contributing approximately 16-17% of total sales and revenue. No single region dominates, indicating balanced global performance.

• Top 5 models by average price are 7 Series (75,570), 3 Series (75,566), i8 (75,366), 5 Series (75,288), and X1 (75,262). Prices are tightly clustered (74,400-75,600), suggesting consistent premium positioning across the lineup.

• Fuel type sales are balanced: Petrol (63.3M), Hybrid (64.5M), Electric (63.2M), Diesel (62.4M). Hybrid leads slightly, while Diesel trails. This indicates successful diversification away from traditional Diesel dominance.

• Mileage category revenue is evenly distributed across all four categories (0-50k: 4.72T, 50-100k: 4.77T, 100-150k: 4.73T, 150k+: 4.80T). Higher-mileage vehicles generate slightly more total revenue, suggesting strong used car market performance.

• Engine size revenue shows 2.5L (577B) and 1.9L (576B) as top contributors, followed by 4.2L (571B), 4.7L (565B), and 3.2L (563B). Smaller engines (1.5L: 268B, 5.0L: 264B) contribute least.


RECOMMENDATIONS

* Maintain balanced regional strategy. No single region dominates, so continue equal resource allocation across all six regions.

* Leverage premium pricing, as models are consistently priced (74k-76k). Consider slight increases for top performers (7 Series, 3 Series) to maximize revenue.

* Continue fuel type diversification since Hybrid leads sales; increase Hybrid inventory slightly. Maintain Electric and Petrol as core segments.

* Higher-mileage vehicles (150k+ km) generate the most total revenue (4.80T). Expand certified pre-owned programs for high-mileage vehicles.

* Focus inventory on 2.5L, 1.9L, 4.2L, 4.7L, and 3.2L engines (combined 2.85T revenue). Reduce inventory of 1.5L and 5.0L engines (combined 0.53T revenue).

* Monitor 2022 peak performance: Both revenue (1.34T) and sales volume (17.9M) peaked in 2022. Analyze factors behind this peak (new models, marketing campaigns, etc.) and replicate strategies.


CONCLUSION

BMW demonstrates balanced global performance with consistent premium pricing and successful fuel type diversification. Hybrid leads sales, while higher-mileage vehicles generate strong used car revenue. Mid-size engines (2.5L, 1.9L, 4.2L) are top revenue contributors. By maintaining regional balance, optimizing engine size inventory, capitalizing on used car strength, and learning from the 2022 peak, BMW can sustain growth and maximize revenue across all markets.

In [ ]:
